region_cingulate_anteriorCingulate_L.name: Anterior Cingulate (L)
region_cingulate_anteriorCingulate_L.function: Error detection and conflict monitoring. This is where the error-related potential (ErrP) originates — the involuntary signal NAVADA_9 reads to know your brain disagreed with a machine decision.

region_cingulate_anteriorCingulate_R.name: Anterior Cingulate (R)
region_cingulate_anteriorCingulate_R.function: Error detection and conflict monitoring. Generates the error-related potential (ErrP) that NAVADA_9 captures at the mastoid to close the brain–AI loop.

region_cerebrum_frontalLobe_L.name: Frontal Lobe (L)
region_cerebrum_frontalLobe_L.function: Executive function, planning, decision-making, voluntary movement, language production and personality. Heavily involved in judging whether an AI action was right or wrong.

region_cerebrum_frontalLobe_R.name: Frontal Lobe (R)
region_cerebrum_frontalLobe_R.function: Executive function, planning, decision-making, voluntary movement and personality. Works with the anterior cingulate during error evaluation.

region_cerebrum_parietalLobe_L.name: Parietal Lobe (L)
region_cerebrum_parietalLobe_L.function: Integrates sensory information, spatial awareness, touch, and navigation. Builds the body's map of the world.

region_cerebrum_parietalLobe_R.name: Parietal Lobe (R)
region_cerebrum_parietalLobe_R.function: Integrates sensory information and spatial attention. Key to perceiving where objects are around you.

region_cerebrum_temporalLobe_L.name: Temporal Lobe (L)
region_cerebrum_temporalLobe_L.function: Hearing, language comprehension, and memory formation. Houses the auditory cortex and supports recognising what you see and hear.

region_cerebrum_temporalLobe_R.name: Temporal Lobe (R)
region_cerebrum_temporalLobe_R.function: Hearing, facial recognition and memory. Processes tone, music and emotional context of sound.

region_cerebrum_occipitalLobe_L.name: Occipital Lobe (L)
region_cerebrum_occipitalLobe_L.function: The primary visual cortex. Processes everything you see — colour, motion and shape.

region_cerebrum_occipitalLobe_R.name: Occipital Lobe (R)
region_cerebrum_occipitalLobe_R.function: Visual processing of the opposite field of view. Turns raw light into recognisable images.

region_cerebrum_insula_L.name: Insula (L)
region_cerebrum_insula_L.function: Interoception (sensing the body's internal state), emotion, and the gut feeling that something is wrong.

region_cerebrum_insula_R.name: Insula (R)
region_cerebrum_insula_R.function: Awareness of internal bodily signals, risk and discomfort. Contributes to the instinctive 'no' response.

region_cerebellum_L.name: Cerebellum (L)
region_cerebellum_L.function: Coordinates movement, balance, timing and motor learning. Makes actions smooth and precise.

region_cerebellum_R.name: Cerebellum (R)
region_cerebellum_R.function: Fine-tunes motor control and balance, and supports rapid, well-timed reactions.

region_cerebellum_vermis.name: Cerebellar Vermis
region_cerebellum_vermis.function: Midline cerebellum governing posture, gait and trunk stability.

region_brainstem_midbrain.name: Midbrain
region_brainstem_midbrain.function: Relays vision and hearing reflexes, controls eye movement, and regulates arousal and alertness.

region_brainstem_pons.name: Pons
region_brainstem_pons.function: Bridges the brain and cerebellum; regulates breathing, sleep and facial signals.

region_brainstem_medulla.name: Medulla Oblongata
region_brainstem_medulla.function: Controls automatic vital functions: heartbeat, breathing and blood pressure.

region_diencephalon_thalamus_L.name: Thalamus (L)
region_diencephalon_thalamus_L.function: The brain's relay station — routes nearly all sensory and motor signals to the cortex and gates attention.

region_diencephalon_thalamus_R.name: Thalamus (R)
region_diencephalon_thalamus_R.function: Relays sensory and motor information to the cortex and regulates consciousness and alertness.

region_limbic_hippocampus_L.name: Hippocampus (L)
region_limbic_hippocampus_L.function: Forms new memories and consolidates learning. Strongly impaired by sleep loss.

region_limbic_hippocampus_R.name: Hippocampus (R)
region_limbic_hippocampus_R.function: Spatial memory and navigation; converts short-term experience into long-term memory.

region_limbic_amygdala_L.name: Amygdala (L)
region_limbic_amygdala_L.function: Threat detection and emotional response. Becomes hyperactive when tired or stressed.

region_limbic_amygdala_R.name: Amygdala (R)
region_limbic_amygdala_R.function: Processes fear, emotional salience and rapid emotional reactions.

region_basalGanglia_caudate_L.name: Caudate Nucleus (L)
region_basalGanglia_caudate_L.function: Goal-directed action, habit and reward learning. Helps select and reinforce correct behaviour.

region_basalGanglia_putamen_L.name: Putamen (L)
region_basalGanglia_putamen_L.function: Regulates movement and motor skill learning, working with the cortex to refine actions.

region_basalGanglia_putamen_R.name: Putamen (R)
region_basalGanglia_putamen_R.function: Controls movement amplitude and automatic motor sequences.

region_basalGanglia_globusPallidus_L.name: Globus Pallidus (L)
region_basalGanglia_globusPallidus_L.function: Modulates and smooths voluntary movement, preventing unwanted motion.

region_basalGanglia_globusPallidus_R.name: Globus Pallidus (R)
region_basalGanglia_globusPallidus_R.function: Final output of the basal ganglia, fine-tuning motor commands sent to the thalamus.

region_connective_corpusCallosum.name: Corpus Callosum
region_connective_corpusCallosum.function: The bridge of ~200 million fibres connecting the two hemispheres so they share information.

region_diencephalon_hypothalamus.name: Hypothalamus
region_diencephalon_hypothalamus.function: Master regulator of sleep, hunger, temperature, hormones and the sleep–wake cycle.
